description: Auto-detect source language: Add a new module src/stx/lang_detect.py that uses the langdetect library to inspect source strings (label column) and estimate the source language. Integrate into CLI, GUI Phase 1, and GUI Phase 2.
steps: 1. Add 'langdetect>=1.0.9,<2' to [project.optional-dependencies] in pyproject.toml under a new 'detect' extra, AND also add it to the 'gui' and 'dev' extras so it is available during testing and GUI usage.

2. Create src/stx/lang_detect.py with: (a) a function detect_source_language(texts: list[str], top_n: int = 3) -> list[tuple[str, float]] that samples up to 50 non-empty source strings, runs langdetect on them, aggregates results, and returns the top_n detected languages as (language_code, confidence) tuples. (b) a function map_detected_to_salesforce(detected_code: str) -> str | None that maps the ISO 639-1 code from langdetect to the closest Salesforce language code using the LANGUAGE_NAME_TO_CODE/CODE_TO_LANGUAGE_NAME mappings. (c) Handle ImportError gracefully if langdetect is not installed (return empty list). (d) Handle multi-language detection: if no single language exceeds 60% confidence, return all candidates and let the caller decide.

3. Add a --detect-source / --no-detect-source flag to the 'stx translate' and 'stx run' CLI commands in src/stx/cli.py. When enabled (default: enabled), call detect_source_language on doc.entries labels, print the detected language suggestion, and use it as the source language unless --source is explicitly provided. Print a Rich info line like 'Detected source language: English (en) [confidence: 92%]'.

4. In src/stx/gui/pages/phase1_import.py, after parsing the STF (_on_parsed method), call detect_source_language on the document's labels. If a clear winner is detected, auto-populate a new 'Source language' field (add QLineEdit for source language name and code next to the existing target language fields in the metadata grid). Show an info label like 'Auto-detected: English (95%)'. The user can override.
5. In src/stx/gui/pages/phase2_excel.py, when loading an existing workbook (_on_loaded), similarly detect source language from labels and show a suggestion.
6. Store the detected source language in AppState (add source_language_name and source_language_code fields to src/stx/gui/state.py if not already present).

7. Create tests/test_lang_detect.py with tests: (a) detect English text, (b) detect Japanese text, (c) detect French text, (d) handle empty input, (e) handle ImportError gracefully (mock langdetect unavailable), (f) test map_detected_to_salesforce mapping, (g) test multi-language mixed input returns multiple candidates.
acceptance_criteria: langdetect is listed in pyproject.toml optional dependencies (detect, gui, dev extras)
src/stx/lang_detect.py exists with detect_source_language() and map_detected_to_salesforce() functions
CLI 'stx translate' and 'stx run' have --detect-source flag and print detected language info
GUI Phase 1 shows detected source language after parsing STF
tests/test_lang_detect.py has at least 7 test cases and all pass
All existing tests continue to pass (pytest -q)
verification: Run: pytest tests/test_lang_detect.py -v to verify all new detection tests pass
Run: pytest -q to confirm no regressions
